Off Calendar Jul-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane.

No appearance was made by Nathan Paco due to a medical condition. There being no oppositions filed, the court adopted the tentative ruling. The Court granted the Motion for Summary Judgment, (signing the order for the same). The Motion For Summary Adjudication was taken off calendar.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. PES-07-289658.

Appearances: Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane (Trustee).

Ms. Erhardt confirmed that the opposition filed November 17, 2008 was the revised opposition, and stated that she would file an errata of the face page. In addition, she stated that she wasn't able to contact Mr. Paco and that she would be filing a Motion for Summary Judgment. All matters were continued to May 4, 2009 which includes status on the filing of the errata.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

Appearances: Betty Hsu-Nordetedt (appearing for Ms. Erhardt) for Zoya Soane.

Ms. Hsu-Nordstedt informed the Court that the Motion For Discovery Sanctions was granted and that she was anticipating filng a Motion for Summary Judgment. All matters were continued to January 26, 2009 for status.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Off Calendar

Notice Of Petitioner Zoya Soanes Motion For Discovery Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Elizabeth T. Erhardt, Esq., for Petitioner and moving party: Zoya Soane
Nathan Paco Martynenko, Respondent and opposing party in Propria Persona

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Petitioner Zoya Soane's motion for evidentiary and monetary sanctions and for an order to deem certain matters admitted is granted. The Court will exercise its discretion and not consider the late-filed opposition. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $940 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Amended Petition To Determine Revocability Of Trust
Off Calendar Sep-29-2008 Continued To Oct-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nathan Paco for Zinaida Martynenko. Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane (Trustee).

Mr. Paco stated that due to some medical problems he had been unable to file a substitution of attorney, but stated he would have it on file by the end of the week. Ms. Erhardt expressed concerns regarding Mr. Paco's standing to represent Ms. Martynenko. Upon the Court's inquiry, Mr. Paco stated he was currently licensed to practice, that he did have written authorization from his client, and noted his client would be filing a supplemental petition with additional causes of action. Ms. Erhardt stated that she would oppose the amendment. The Court instructed Mr. Paco to file his substitution of attorney and instructed Ms. Erhardt to submit the proposed order from the September 15, 2008 hearing for signature. The matter was continued to October 14, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/ R.Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane.

The Court stated that the the proof of service issue had been resolved and that the matter could proceed. The Court found that the Petition did not violate the no contest clause and therefore granted the petition subject to a written order. Ms. Erhardt stated that she had no information regarding Zinaida being represented by counsel. The matter was set for September 29, 2008 for Zoya Soane's filing of a revised opposition.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera.

Notice Of Petitioner Zoya Soane'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Admissions;
Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Elizabeth T. Erhardt, Esq., for petitioner and moving party: Zoya Soane

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Petitioner Zoya Soane's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and requests for admissions and attendance at deposition is granted. No opposition has been filed. Respondent is ordered to serve further verified responses within 15 days of notice of this order. Respondent is also ordered to contact counsel for petitioner to schedule the deposition at a mutually convenient time. The deposition shall be completed within 30 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $727.50 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Appearances: Elvira Orly for Zina Martynenko (Surviving Spouse). Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane (Trustee).

The Court stated that there were no oppositions to the Motion to be Relieved as Counsel based on the irreconcilable differences and granted the Motion on both cases. The Amended Petition to Determine Revocability of Trust was continued to July 14, 2008 for proper notice of Ms. Erhardt oppostion.

Petition For Declaratory Relief For Determination That Zinaida Martynenko Violated The "No-Contest" Clause Of Decedent'S Will And Family Trusts
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elvira Orly for Zina Martynenko (Surviving Spouse). Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane (Trustee).

Ms. Ernhardt stated her intention to file her new contest petition/opposition and a new declaratory relief petition. The amended petition to determine revocability was continued to March 10, 2008. The petition for declaratory relief was denied without prejudice as to raising the issue in other form. Ms. Erhardt will file her new petition for declaratory relief by December 21, 2007 which is to be set for hearing on March 10, 2008.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Raymond Leung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Amended Petition To Determine Revocability Of Trust
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Continued To Mar-10-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination That Proposed Opposition To Zinaida Martynenko'S Petition Does Not Violate "No-Contest" Clauses Of The Family Trusts And Will
MINI MINUTES: Called with case #289658 (Estate of Vladimir Martynenko)
Appearances: Virginia Palmer and Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane. Elvira Orly for the 2nd wife (Zena). Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

In the Trust case:
The Petition for Determination That Proposed Opposition To Zinaida Martynenko's Petition Does Not Violate "No Contest" Clause of The Family Trust and Will was granted. Ms. Palmer presented arguement regarding how Probate Code 21320 applies to her petition and would like time to file a breif in support. The Court continued the matter to December 10, 2007. Ms. Palmer is to file her brief by November 28, 2007. Ms. Orly is to file her response by December 3, 2007.

All the matters in the estate case were continued to May 19, 2008. There will no further actions in the probate matter until the Trust matters are resolved. Status Reports are to be filed two weeks prior. The SFPA will continue to act as the Special Administrator. Ms. Palmer will prepare orders for both matters.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mizota.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Amended Petition To Determine Revocability Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Continued To Dec-10-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

For Delaratory Relief For Determination That Zinaida Martynenko Violated The 'No-Contest' Clause Of Decedent Vladimir Martynenko'S Will,Family Trust % Sub-Trusts [Probate Code 21320 Et Seq.]
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Off Calendar

Petition For Declaratory Relief For Determination That Zinaida Martynenko Violated The "No-Contest" Clause Of Decedent'S Will And Family Trusts
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Continued To Dec-10-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Set Aside Conveyances To Trust, Etc.
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Line #52 case #289658 (Estate of Vladimir Martynenko). Jonathan Ferdon appeared for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Elvira Orly appeared for the second wife. Virginia Palmer appeared for Zoya Soane. Emily Warren appeared for Zinaida Martynenko.

Mr. Ferdon requested to have the letters extended unitl the appointment of the personal representative, which the Court granted. Ms. Palmer informed the Court that there are 3 Petitions for Declaratory Relief on today and 2 on for 11/19/07. She reported that the 2 being heard on the Trust set for 11/19/07 are to be determined first and that the 3 that are scheduled for today can wait until then. Ms Palmer requested that the 2 petitions for probate be continued. The Court indicated that the 1991 will is ready to go and that the other probate matter is still waiting for the Proof of Subscribing Witness.

The Petition To Set Aside Conveyances to Trust is to be taken off calendar. The amended Petition To Determine Revokability of Trust is to remain on calendar and continued to 11/19/07. All petitions were continued to 11/19/07.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR#11332. Bailiff: Rich Lee. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.
